content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 8, 1988

Mark Shusted
Research Attorney
Superior Court
County of Monterey
P.O. Box 414
Salinas, CA 93902

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-88-080

Dear Mr. Shusted:

I am writing to confirm the substance of our telephone conversations of February 17 and 22, 1988. You wanted to know whether the Commission requires adoption of conflict of interest codes for grand juries and the basis for this requirement.

Government Code Section 87300 requires every agency to adopt a conflict of interest code. Any state or local government agency is an "agency" for the purposes of the Political Reform Act.1/ (Section 82003.) Section 82041 describes a local government agency as a "department, division, bureau, office, board, commission or other agency of the foregoing."

The California Supreme Court in People v. Superior Court (1975) 13 Cal. 3d 430, 438, found a grand jury to be a judicial body that is "part of the court by which it is convened." Therefore, because a grand jury is a department or agency of a court, the grand jury is subject to the provisions of Section 87300.

_________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

MR. Shuster
March 8, 1988
Page 2

     The Commission considered this issue at some length in
1986. I enclose a copy of the Commission's Whittington Advice
Letter, No. A-85-185, which discusses the Commission's legal
interpretation in more detail. At the subsequent Commission
hearings mentioned in Whittington, the Commission endorsed the
arguments set out in the letter, but also concluded that the
counties are in the best position to evaluate the varying
duties and powers of local grand juries and to assign
appropriate disclosure categories.

     I hope this clarifies the basis for Commission authority to
require conflict of interest codes for grand juries. Please
call me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any questions about this
letter.


                                        Sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: Margarita Altamirano
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:MA:mk

sections.question: whether the Commission requires adoption of conflict of interest codes for grand juries and the basis for this requirement
sections.conclusion: Government Code section 87300 requires every agency to adopt a conflict of interest code. A grand jury is a department or agency of a court and therefore is subject to the provisions of section 87300.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the FPPC require grand juries to adopt conflict of interest codes, and what is the legal basis for such a requirement?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Yes, grand juries must adopt conflict of interest codes because they are departments or agencies of courts, which are subject to Government Code section 87300's requirement that every agency adopt such codes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard FPPC advice letter format with clear question and conclusion. References prior Whittington Advice Letter A-85-185 for additional detail. File number I-88-080 indicates incoming request designation.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: whether the Commission requires adoption of conflict of interest codes for grand juries and the basis for this requirement

CONCLUSION: Government Code section 87300 requires every agency to adopt a conflict of interest code. A grand jury is a department or agency of a court and therefore is subject to the provisions of section 87300.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms that grand juries are required to adopt conflict of interest codes pursuant to Government Code section 87300, as grand juries are judicial bodies that constitute departments of courts and thus qualify as agencies under the Political Reform Act.
